# AegisGate Lens Icons

The manifest references four PNG icons (Chrome Web Store requirement):

- `icon-16.png`  (16×16)   — toolbar icon
- `icon-32.png`  (32×32)   — Windows taskbar / Mac dock
- `icon-48.png`  (48×48)   — extension management page
- `icon-128.png` (128×128) — Web Store listing, install dialog

## Where these files come from

**The PNGs in `dist/icons/` are generated by the build tool**, not committed
to this repo and not generated from `src/icons/` directly. The build tool
lives at `consolidated/aegisgate-platform/tools/build-lens-extension/`.

The source for the icon is `lens-icon-source.png`, a sibling of the build
tool's Go source. It is byte-identical to the website logo at
`websites/aegisgate-site/public/logo.png`. (If you want to update the
Lens icon, edit the website logo and copy it over.)

The build tool auto-crops transparent / white padding to a square, then
nearest-neighbor-resizes to 16, 32, 48, 128 px.

## Why this README exists

This `README.md` IS committed to the repo (in `src/icons/`). It gets
copied to `dist/icons/` by the build tool's `bundle` stage, and it gets
zipped into the extension. Chrome ignores non-icon files in `icons/`,
so the README is harmless in the published extension, but it's useful
in source control as a pointer for future contributors.

## Verification

To regenerate the icons locally:

```bash
cd consolidated/aegisgate-platform
go run ./tools/build-lens-extension/ \
    --src ../../lens-repo-bootstrap/src \
    --dist /tmp/lens-FINAL28 \
    --version 0.1.0 \
    --commit v0
ls /tmp/lens-FINAL28/icons/
# Should see: icon-16.png, icon-32.png, icon-48.png, icon-128.png
```
